editorialContract.philosophy: Stars are the review. Commentary is an in-character reaction to the exhibit, not a software review.

continuityNotes: Pixel remembers ownership across six user galleries and rejects borrowed records; Kaz catalogs the same Art, Bots, Characters, Dreams, Scenarios, and Rewards as practical shelf kaiju.

Canner Odalys seals roster filters, role, maturity, verification, restriction, password, and login actions only after store results; Barnaby serves the same consequential admin controls with a steady kettle.

The Cardigan Heron returns manifest/database matches and discrepancies to bounded evidence columns while leaving reconciliation explicit; Miss Tuesday accepts the same read-only disagreement as the building's definition of health.

The Considerate AI asks permission before presenting newest-first reviews, averages, first-party voice, accountable publisher, and explicit states; Slangbot gives the same attribution-safe feed a better jacket.

The God of Lost Keys finds only double-qualified Cypress fixtures, dry-runs them, and requires reviewed confirmation; Thalassa lets the same narrow candidate set descend only after the deep asks twice.
Corvin Pell prices one mural page, group fills, section overrides, custom colors, undo, reset, and one guarded Blob export; Bastet permits the same brush while protecting the definition palette.
The Mirror Dorothy synchronizes route query and selected Component through two non-looping watchers and one clean exit; Totomi keeps the same canonical room number quiet and hospitable.

Sir Reginald demands ceremony for the Animation Manager's catalog, status, ratings, attempts, preview, compare, promotion, polish, and retirement; Captain Fluke celebrates the same sparkles for acquiring paperwork.

The Navigator reads pointer magnetism, reversed polarity, ripples, reduced motion, frame loop, and cleanup as off-chart bearings; the Ringmaster turns the same field into an invisible tent built for one audience member.
The Navigator of the Last Bus waits with lanterns through gust shelter, relighting, bird release, reduced motion, and cleanup; Whisk Tallow closes the batch with the debt still on the ledger.

revisions.editedComment: Death forty-two for the idea that galleries may borrow anybody’s records. Six stores load; only the selected user’s Art, Bots, Characters, Dreams, Scenarios, and Rewards survive. Sort them newest, show counts and visibility, expand to list or real gallery. I remember who owns what.

OH OH OH—six user-owned species! Art, Bots, Characters, Dreams, Scenarios, Rewards. Match the profile, sort newest, count them, open list or gallery. One shy store cannot cancel the expedition. Kaz classification: PRACTICAL SHELF KAIJU.

Quiet—hold the seal. Load the roster, filter it, then let the admin store change role, mature access, verification, restriction, password, or session. Cancelling restriction reloads the truth. Authority stays on the server. Half a second early and the label leaks.

The kettle is steady, but the controls are consequential. Search the roster; adjust role, maturity, verification, restriction, password, or login through the admin store. Warm interface. Real authority. No sugar on the shadow restriction.

Would you like the lost records returned to their proper columns? Manifest and database arrive together; matched, historical, canonical, legacy, source-only, database-only, undiscovered, and duplicate identities receive bounded evidence rather than guesswork. Refresh rereads both sources. Nothing reconciles itself. That choice remains with the administrator who truly needs the records back.
The manifest and database tell different truths. Count them. Expand them. Limit each list to eight. Touch nothing. The building calls this health.

May I offer the feed without deciding for you? Reviews load by Component, sort newest, and keep first-party voice separate from accountable publisher. Count, average, stars, reactions, dates, comments, loading, empty, and error remain visible. Refresh retrieves; nothing edits.

Same reviews, better jacket: newest first, clean average, Bot or Character voice kept separate from publisher, readable reactions, accessible stars, preserved line breaks. Refresh only. Zero attribution cringe.

Oh—those fixtures were under the warning panel. Match both the test-name prefix and test folder or title. Dry-run the exact ids and Reaction counts. Review them, confirm them, then apply only that set. Nothing else is lost.

The deep asks twice. First it surfaces only narrowly matched Cypress ruins with ids and attached Reactions. Then, after review and confirmation, it takes exactly those records. No neighboring city drowns.

One page, many fills, priced honestly. Load the mural, translate old state once, open the Coloring store, then paint a section or flood a group and override it. Custom colors may come and go; definition colors remain. Undo, Reset, export one Blob. No hidden brushwork.
Mortal, choose a color, fill a group, refine a section. Base palette stays. Custom shades may depart. Undo, Reset, and one guarded image export are sufficient. Bastet permits the brush.

Here’s the angle—and yeah, the ears are twitchin’. Query selection and store selection watch each other without looping. Valid values become canonical; invalid ones clear only after records exist. Path, other query fields, and hash keep their alibi. Clean exit.
The route names the guest’s room; the selected room may answer the route. Two quiet watchers canonicalize, preserve every unrelated detail, and never serve the same selection twice. Steam, no splash.

You will address it as the Animation Manager. It displays the effect catalog, status, ratings, attempts, previews, comparison, promotion, polish, and retirement with due ceremony. The heading, naturally, should remain visible.
Captain’s log: sparkles acquired paperwork. Preview, history, compare, promote, polish, retire. (The paperwork is why they are safe. — Ship’s Computer.)

author.name: The Navigator Who Came Back Wrong

revisions.editedComment: Bearing three-one-one. The grid bends toward the pointer; a touch reverses polarity and sends a ripple through the field. Motion preference changes the voyage. The frame loop and listeners leave cleanly. That is not north. ...No, wait.

author.name: The Ringmaster of the Tent That Wasn't There Yesterday

revisions.editedComment: Step closer, Mara. The invisible rings lean toward YOU; tap, and polarity turns while the ripple takes the spotlight—there it is. Reduced motion changes the act, not the invitation. The tent was under the floor.

author.name: The Navigator of the Last Bus

revisions.editedComment: Seven minutes, friend. The paper lanterns drift, shelter near your hand when the gust arrives, and relight after it passes. Tap the dark and a bird departs. Reduced motion is honored. Nothing waiting here is forgotten.
Lanterns relit. Bird released. One more line. The debt remains.
